title: Witch of Many Colors
description: A Fabric Modpack based around Bewitchment, Spectrum, and the RPG Series, with Immersive Portals and Gravity for fun.
body: Based around Bewitchment, Spectrum, and the RPG Series, with Immersive Portals, Gravity, and Bits and Chisels for fun with sizes, this pack explores some of the unusual mods in Fabric. # Note about 1.0.4 update: The 1.0.3 pack version had a modified pack name. Fixing that in the 1.0.4 pack update apparently makes Modrinth install a separate profile. I don't know why this happens, as the pack id should be the same. To handle this, copy everything except the mods/ folder from the old profile to the new one. # Important Facts: * The biggest progression mods are Bewitchment (a rebuild of Witchery) and Spectrum (like ChromatiCraft) * Each of the RPG Series classes has spell books, and some have additional important items * Any RPG Series weapon can cast any RPG series spells (hammer cast fireball, etc.) * The biggest exploration mods are Wilder Wilds, Promenade, BetterNether, and BetterEnd * Hover over each inventory slot to see additional Trinkets slots (a terrible interface) * Put a lantern on your belt for hands-free dynamic lighting ## Portals * The Immersive Portals mod exists, use the Portal Wand to create and move portals * Use /portal delete_portal four times, once from each direction, to fully delete a portal * The Gravity mod exists, use /portal set_portal_nbt {teleportChangesGravity:true} looking at a portal * Portals that connect different sizes will shrink you, use /scale set 1 to reset ## Fun Items * Use a Spyglass to create constellations * Put banners on horses, which have some improvements and enchantable armor * Use a Scarf, a Scarf Stapler, and pieces of wool to make long scarves * You can make multi-block elevators (search in JEI for "Elevator") * There are multiple kinds of hammers, including weapons and 3x3 mining, choose carefully * The Bits and Chisels mod exists * There are Magnum Torches and Building Wands * Use Scythes to right-click harvest in an area, and replant ## Additional Notes * You can jump over fences * You can right-click things like ingots on the ground to place them * There are cosmetic armor slots and a menu for making your armor partially transparent * The /changedimension and /wits commands exist * Better Combat is recommended by the RPG Series, so it's been included * Gazebos probably doesn't work w/ Luki's Grand Capitals, but keeping it in just in case Icon made with Font generator # Setup guide for servers: ## Disable Universal Gravestones There's a server-only bug involving Universal Gravestones. It's probably an incompatibility with Immersive Portals. Removing the last item from your grave kicks you with DecoderException: IndexOutOfBoundsException, and you can't log in again for about a minute. Recommendation: disable Universal Gravestones on the server and /gamerule keepInventory true. Clients do not need to disable the mod. # Pickaxe and Material Tiers ## Pickaxes #### Tier 0 (Wood / can mine Stone) * Wood Pickaxe - Minecraft (vanilla) #### Tier 1 (Stone / can mine Iron Ore) * Stone Pickaxe - Minecraft (vanilla) * Copper Pickaxe - Mythic Metals #### Tier 2 (Iron / can mine Diamond Ore) * Iron Pickaxe - Minecraft (vanilla) * Aquarium Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Banglum Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Bronze Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Quadrillum Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Thallasium Pickaxe - Better End (unusual construction) * Cincinnasite Pickaxe - Better Nether #### Tier 3 (Diamond / can mine Obsidian) * Diamond Pickaxe - Minecraft (vanilla) * Resonarium Pickaxe - Deeper and Darker (must be found?) * Carmot Pickaxe - Mythic Metals (extra effect) * Durasteel Pickaxe - Mythic Metals (must be found?) * Kyber Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Legendary Banglum Pickaxe - Mythic Metals (extra effect) * Osmium Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Prometheum Pickaxe - Mythic Metals (extra effect) * Runite Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Steel Pickaxe - Mythic Metals (must be found?) * Stormyx Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Tidesinger Pickaxe - Mythic Metals (extra effect) * Terminite Pickaxe - Better End (unusual construction) * Nether Ruby Pickaxe - Better Nether (extra effect) * Cincinnasite-Diamond Pickaxe - Better Nether (extra effect) #### Tier 4 (Netherite / can mine some modded blocks like Unobtainium) (Some of these might actually be tier 5, but I don't have a way to check that) * Netherite Pickaxe - Minecraft (vanilla) * Warden Pickaxe - Deeper and Darker * Adamantite Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Celestium Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Hallowed Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Metallurgium Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Mythril Drill - Mythic Metals (has fuel and upgrades) * Mythril Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Orichalcum Pickaxe - Mythic Metals * Palladium Pickaxe - Mythic Metals (extra effect) * Star Platinum Pickaxe - Mythic Metals (must be found?) * Aeternium Pickaxe - Better End (unusual construction) * Fire Ruby Pickaxe - Better Nether (extra effect) ## Materials #### Tier 0 (can be mined by Wood Pickaxe) * Minecraft (vanilla): all stone blocks: Stone, Calcite, Diorite, etc. * Bewitchment: Salt Ore * Better End: Ender Ore, Amber Ore, Thallasium Ore #### Tier 1 (can be mined by Stone Pickaxe) * Minecraft (vanilla): many ores: Iron Ore, Copper Ore, etc. * Mythic Metals: Manganese Ore, Midas Gold Ore, Silver Ore, Tin Ore (not used) #### Tier 2 (can be mined by Iron Pickaxe) * Minecraft (vanilla): several ores: Diamond Ore, Gold Ore, etc. * Bewitchment: Silver Ore * Mythic Metals: Aquarium Ore, Banglum Ore, Carmot Ore, Kyber Ore, Morkite Ore, Osmium Ore, Platinum Ore, Prometheum Ore, Quadrillum Ore, Runite Ore, Stormyx Ore * Jewelry (RPG Series): Gem Vein * Better Nether: Cincinnasite Ore #### Tier 3 (can be mined by Diamond Pickaxe) * Minecraft (vanilla): Obsidian, Crying Obsidian, etc. * Mythic Metals: Adamantite Ore, Mythril Ore, Orichalcum Ore, Palladium Ore, Starrite Ore (not used) * Better Nether: Nether Ruby Ore #### Tier 4 (can be mined by Netherite Pickaxe) * Minecraft (vanilla): nothing requires this level * Mythic Metals: Unobtainium Ore
